tryemsense-adv3.snippet: If You're Over 50 With Burning or Numb Feet, You're Probably Making at Least 3 of These 5 Mistakes

tryemsense-adv3.text: New research into peripheral neuropathy reveals why common remedies fail — and the clinical protocol that targets what's actually killing your nerves.
Layer 1 — Constricted blood vessels restrict fresh blood from reaching your feet. ‍ Layer 2 — Stagnant fluid and swelling block what little circulation remains. ‍ Layer 3 — Dormant nerve tissue stops

Stage 1 — Intermittent tingling (nerves signaling distress) ‍ Stage 2 — Persistent burning (nerve sheath degrading) ‍ Stage 3 — Spreading numbness (nerve function shutting down) ‍ Stage 4 — Loss of ba
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Carol K., 63, Phoenix — diabetic neuropathy 4 years: ‍ "After 3 weeks, I could feel the carpet under my feet for the first time in 2 years. I stood there in my living room and cried."
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Robert A., 58, Detroit — tried everything: "I'd tried gabapentin, PT, even acupuncture. This was the first thing that went after what was actually causing the numbness."
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Margaret T., 55, Tampa — lost active life: "I went from canceling plans every weekend to walking 2 miles with my daughter last Sunday. My husband says I'm a different person."
